rationale: This is the classic Skraup synthesis, reacting aniline with glycerol under acidic conditions to form the parent quinoline heterocycle. Glycerol dehydrates in situ to acrolein, which then undergoes a Michael addition with aniline, followed by cyclization and oxidation. This example is the simplest and most fundamental representation of the reaction family.
collision_avoidance_note: The reaction uses simple, non-functionalized aniline and glycerol to produce the unsubstituted quinoline core. This avoids complex substituents, arene-rich structures, or other reactive functional groups that could overlap with other named reactions involving anilines or heterocycle synthesis. The use of glycerol as the three-carbon source is characteristic of the Skraup variant.
